skill.name: spreadsheet-ops
skill.description: Spreadsheet processing and analysis for CSV/Excel; trigger when users ask to merge/clean tabular data, run statistics, add/edit Excel formulas, apply formatting, generate charts, or force workbook recalculation.
skill.summary: Automate spreadsheet merging, cleaning, analysis, formulas, formatting, and chart generation with Python scripts.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 62 static analysis findings are false positives. The 24 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections result from the scanner misidentifying pandas describe(), CONFIG dictionaries, and markdown text as cryptographic operations. The 32 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are Excel formula placeholder syntax (e.g., =B{row}*C{row}) in documentation, not shell commands. The skill is a legitimate spreadsheet processing tool using standard libraries (pandas, openpyxl) with no malicious intent. Previous audit graded it 'Production Ready' with 85/100. Risk level is LOW - safe to publish with standard awareness that the skill executes Python scripts for file operations.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 24 locations as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' but none contain cryptographic operations. Findings cover pandas describe(), CONFIG dictionaries, markdown text, and JSON audit data. All are false positives.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 32 instances as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are Excel formula placeholder patterns like =B{row}*C{row} in documentation references, and Python attribute assignment (fullCalcOnLoad = True) in recalc.md. No shell execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 12 locations as 'System reconnaissance'. Content includes column normalization rules, file alias examples, and JSON audit data. No reconnaissance behavior detected.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged column-matching.md:13 as 'Network reconnaissance'. This line contains a column alias example 'email: e-mail, email_address' which is a legitimate data mapping pattern.

content.user_title: Process and Analyze Spreadsheets with AI
content.value_statement: Manually merging, cleaning, and analyzing CSV and Excel files is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates tabular data operations including merging, deduplication, formula creation, formatting, and chart generation using Python scripts.
actual_capabilities: Merge multiple CSV and Excel files with column normalization and alignment
Clean tabular data through deduplication, conflict resolution, and header normalization
Compute descriptive statistics and group-by analysis on spreadsheet data
Add and edit Excel formulas across specified cell ranges
Apply cell formatting and conditional formatting rules to Excel worksheets
Generate bar, line, and pie charts from worksheet data ranges
limitations: Cannot calculate formula results directly; requires Excel to recalculate on open
CSV files must be converted to Excel before applying formulas, formatting, or charts
Large files over 100MB may require chunked processing strategies for performance
Chart generation supports only bar, line, and pie chart types

use_cases.title: Merge Sales Data from Multiple Regions
use_cases.description: Combine CSV and Excel files from different departments or regions into a single dataset with normalized column names and deduplicated rows.

use_cases.title: Generate Statistical Reports from Survey Data
use_cases.description: Compute descriptive statistics, grouped summaries, and outlier detection on survey or experimental data exported as CSV or Excel.

use_cases.title: Automate Excel Report Formatting and Charting
use_cases.description: Apply consistent formatting rules and generate bar or line charts for monthly business reports without manual spreadsheet manipulation.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Two CSV Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Merge the files data1.csv and data2.csv using the email column as the primary key. Normalize column names and output the result as merged_output.xlsx.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has two CSV files with customer data that need to be combined into one Excel file.

prompt_templates.title: Clean and Deduplicate Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Clean the Excel file customers.xlsx. Normalize all column headers to lowercase with underscores, remove duplicate rows based on the email column keeping the first occurrence, and report any conflicting field values.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a messy spreadsheet with inconsistent headers and duplicate entries.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Formulas to an Excel Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Open invoice.xlsx and add a formula to column D starting from row 2 that multiplies column B by column C. Use the pattern =B{row}*C{row} and apply it to all rows with data.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs to add calculated columns to an existing Excel workbook.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Charts with Formatted Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Load sales_report.xlsx, create a line chart from the monthly sales data in range B1:B12 with dates from A1:A12 as categories. Apply currency formatting to column C and set column widths for readability.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a polished Excel report with both charts and professional formatting.

output_examples.input: Merge three CSV files containing customer records from different sources into one clean Excel file.
output: An Excel file with all customer records combined, duplicate rows removed based on email address, and columns renamed consistently (e.g., 'First Name', 'firstname', 'fname' all mapped to 'first_name').
A conflict report listing any records where the same email had different phone numbers or company names across source files.

output_examples.input: Analyze the experiment_results.xlsx file and generate summary statistics.
output: An Excel file with two sheets: a 'summary' sheet containing count, mean, min, max, and quartiles for each numeric column, and a 'sample' sheet showing the first 100 data rows.
A text output showing the top 10 groups by total value when grouped by the specified category column.

output_examples.input: Apply formatting and a chart to the quarterly report spreadsheet.
output: An Excel file with bold headers, centered text, currency formatting on financial columns, and column widths adjusted for readability.
A line chart added to the worksheet visualizing the revenue trend across the specified data range.

best_practices: Always confirm input and output file paths, formats, and worksheet names before running scripts to avoid overwriting data.
Configure the CONFIG dictionary at the top of each script with explicit file paths and parameters before execution.
For large files over 100MB, enable chunked reading and convert intermediate results to Parquet format for better performance.
anti_patterns: Attempting to apply Excel-only operations like formulas or formatting to CSV files without first converting to .xlsx format.
Hard-coding absolute file paths in CONFIG that will not exist in the user's environment; always use relative paths or confirm paths with the user.
Skipping column alias mapping when merging files with inconsistent column names, which leads to data loss or misalignment.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: CSV and Excel (.xlsx / .xls) for input and output. Merging and analysis work with both formats. Formulas, formatting, charts, and recalculation require Excel format.

faq.question: Can this skill calculate Excel formula results?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses openpyxl which writes formula strings to cells but does not compute them. Formulas recalculate when the file is opened in Excel.

faq.question: How are duplicate rows handled during merge?
faq.answer: You can choose from four strategies: keep the first record, keep the last record, keep the record with the most complete data, or keep all duplicate records.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Google Sheets?
faq.answer: No. This skill works with local CSV and Excel files only. Google Sheets files must be exported to Excel or CSV format first.

faq.question: What chart types are available?
faq.answer: Three chart types are supported: bar charts, line charts, and pie charts. Charts are embedded in Excel worksheets at a specified position.

faq.question: How are column name conflicts resolved when merging files?
faq.answer: Column names are normalized by lowercasing, trimming spaces, and replacing hyphens and spaces with underscores. You can define explicit alias mappings to handle different naming conventions across source files.
